zh.lifeSummary: 當James Richard Biggs於1530年出生時，他的父親Richard Bigge51歲，母親Joan Hendley45歲。他於1556年與Anne Bigge在英國英格蘭肯特可藍別盧可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1563年享年33歲。

nameMeanings.name: Biggs
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Bigg , with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bigg
meanings: English: from Middle English bigge ‘large, strong, stout’, presumably a descriptive nickname for a large, strong person.
English: topographic name from Middle English big ‘building’, a derivative of Middle English biggen (Old Norse byggja) ‘to build’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of German   Bick   or of some other similar (like-sounding) name from continental Europe (compare   Big   2).

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When James Richard Biggs was born in 1530, his father, Richard Bigge, was 51 and his mother, Joan Hendley, was 45. He married Anne Bigge in 1556, in Cranbrook, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1563, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Richard Biggs est né en 1530, son père, Richard Bigge, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Joan Hendley, avait 45 ans. Il a épousé Anne Bigge en 1556, à Cranbrook, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1563, à l’âge de 33 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Richard Biggs 1530 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Bigge 51 und seine Mutter Joan Hendley 45. Er hat 1556 in Cranbrook, Kent, England, United Kingdom Anne Bigge geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1563 im Alter von 33 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Richard Biggs è nato il nel 1530, il suo padre, Richard Bigge, aveva 51 anni e il suo madre, Joan Hendley, aveva 45 anni. Lui sposato Anne Bigge nel 1556, nel Cranbrook, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1563, all'età di 33 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: James Richard Biggs(이)가 1530년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Bigge(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Hendley(은)는 45세였다. 그는 1556년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 크랜브룩에서 Anne Bigge(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1563년 33세의 나이로 사망했다.
